title: Marine Le Pen to find out whether she can run for president
subhead: A court will rule on Tuesday in Marine Le Pen's appeal trial over alleged misappropriation of European Union funds. Its decision will determine whether she can run in the 2027 presidential election.

title: Explosions rock Damascus during Macron's visit

As US disengages from NATO, Europe's militaries ramp up scattered and unsynchronized
subhead: In Ankara on July 7 and 8, European leaders from NATO want to show that they can cope with reduced US involvement and boost their defense capabilities to respond to any attack. While efforts are now underway, Europe is still struggling to coordinate them.

title: Netanyahu government's legal coup challenges Israel's justice system: 'The final nail in the coffin of democracy'
subhead: By declaring he would not comply with a Supreme Court ruling, Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu is directly confronting the country's highest legal authority, plunging Israel into a constitutional crisis.

title: One hundred early works by Claude Monet reunited at Le Havre's André Malraux Museum
subhead: The MuMa exhibition, showcasing drawings, caricatures and paintings, places the painter in his family and regional context.

title: France faces new heatwave with temperatures reaching 40°C
subhead: Heat swept across much of the country on Monday, with temperatures reaching up to 38°C and even 40°C in the southwest. The third major heatwave of the year is expected to be both intense and prolonged, likely lasting around 12 days.

Somaliland's high-risk quest for recognition
subhead: The self-proclaimed republic aspires to accede to the international stage. It finds itself at the center of a game of alliances, caught between Israeli, American and Emirati ambitions and threats from Yemen and neighboring Somalia.

title: Resigned Le Pen puts on brave face before crucial court decision
subhead: In her final public appearance before the ruling on Tuesday that will determine whether she can run for president, Marine Le Pen focused on highlighting her partnership with Jordan Bardella.

title: 250 years of American independence: How the text that founded the US – and changed the world – was drafted
subhead: 'America 250' (1/13). The 13 colonies had been fighting the British Crown for a year when, in July 1776, lawyer Thomas Jefferson completed the drafting of the Declaration of Independence. Adopted by Congress on July 4 in Philadelphia, this document, which established the principle of equality for all – even though slavery was still legal everywhere – founded the United States of America.
